finding_results.notes: Vel now sets concrete pre-cross spend thresholds, Kem repeats them back, the crossing shows him recognizing and refusing those thresholds in real time, and the aftermath keeps Vel's admitted misread while grounding Kem's failure in his own non-decision.
The accounting now states one total of 18 incidental levels since the Spent Towns, breaks that into 6 before Thessa and 12 since her settlement, and matches the ledger arithmetic to 166 -> 148.
Vel's confrontation no longer uses rhetorical questions or mentor-maxim phrasing; it stays in blunt procedural accusation tied to the river facts and the missed threshold.
Sable is now temporarily separated from the remaining agents for privacy and control, not described as having already sent them back to the capital.
The earlier Kem-internal repetition was removed; only Dren's later line now carries the 'forty years' reference.
The richer first scar-check description remains; the later beat now uses a different gesture so the repeated phrasing is gone.
The bridge description now reconciles the chapter 10 handoff by framing the visible wreckage as flood destruction compounded by long neglect.
The revised chapter is 5108 words, above the 5000-word minimum, with added material concentrated in the river-logic setup, ordeal, and Sable isolation beats.

summary: Reworked the river crossing so Vel's spend threshold is explicit before Kem enters the water, reconciled the incidental accounting to 18 levels since the Spent Towns while preserving Kem at 148, corrected the bridge and Sable-team continuity breaks, removed the flagged repeated phrases, and brought the chapter above the length floor.
